vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-936: Rally feature planning — end-to-end UX and pipeline

plan.title: Rally feature planning: end-to-end UX and pipeline
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Rally Features are non-interactive in the dashboard. FeatureCard has no action bar (Plan/vBRIEF/Tasks), no click-to-detail, and child stories aren't clickable. The backend has no feature-aware planning pipeline — no getChildIssues(), no feature context injection for story workspaces, no cross-story dependency edges.
narratives.Proposal: Phase 1: Add action bar and click-to-select on FeatureCard and CompactChildCard, fix derivedStatus gate. Phase 2: Add getChildIssues() to tracker interface with Rally impl, enhance planning prompt for features, inject FEATURE-CONTEXT.md into story workspaces, write cross-story edges in vBRIEF. Phase 3: Integration tests for all new behavior. Reuse existing planning pipeline (no new feature planning mode). InspectorPanel for detail view. Cross-story edges written but not enforced by Cloister.

items.title: Add action bar to FeatureCard with Plan/See Plan, vBRIEF, and Tasks chips

narrative.Action: Add an action bar to FeatureCard (KanbanBoard.tsx ~line 624) with Plan/See Plan chip, vBRIEF chip, and Tasks chip. Wire chips to existing PlanDialog, VBriefDialog, and BeadsTasksPanel. Explicitly NO Start Agent button — features are planned, not executed directly. The Plan chip should use the same planChip logic as IssueCard but gate visibility on feature's own status (not derivedStatus).

subItems.title: FeatureCard renders action bar with Plan/See Plan, vBRIEF, and Tasks chips below the progress bar
Plan chip opens PlanDialog; vBRIEF chip opens VBriefDialog; Tasks chip opens BeadsTasksPanel — same as IssueCard
No Start Agent button appears on FeatureCard action bar
Plan chip shows 'See Plan' (green) when hasPlan is true, 'Plan' (gray) otherwise

items.title: FeatureCard title click opens InspectorPanel; chevron still toggles expand

narrative.Action: Split FeatureCard click handling: clicking the feature title sets selectedIssue (opens InspectorPanel), clicking the chevron icon toggles expand/collapse. Currently the entire header row toggles expand. Must separate the two click targets so title → select, chevron → expand.

subItems.title: Clicking FeatureCard title text sets selectedIssue and opens InspectorPanel with the feature's data
Clicking the chevron icon toggles expand/collapse without changing selectedIssue
FeatureCard shows selected state (visual highlight) when it is the selectedIssue

items.title: CompactChildCard click selects child story in InspectorPanel

narrative.Action: Make CompactChildCard (KanbanBoard.tsx ~line 719) clickable — clicking the row sets selectedIssue to the child story, opening InspectorPanel. The identifier link currently opens the external tracker; keep that on the identifier specifically, but make the rest of the row select the story.

subItems.title: Clicking a CompactChildCard row (outside the identifier link) sets selectedIssue to the child story
Clicking the identifier link still opens the external tracker in a new tab
CompactChildCard shows selected state when it is the selectedIssue

items.title: InspectorPanel renders feature-appropriate actions (no Start Agent)

narrative.Action: Add feature detection to InspectorPanel (check artifactType includes 'PortfolioItem'). When a feature is selected, render Plan/See Plan, vBRIEF, and Tasks action buttons but NOT Start Agent, Resume Session, or Stop Agent. Features are planning surfaces, not execution surfaces.

subItems.title: InspectorPanel detects features via artifactType containing 'PortfolioItem'
Feature in InspectorPanel shows Plan/See Plan, vBRIEF, and Tasks buttons
Feature in InspectorPanel does NOT show Start Agent, Resume Session, or Stop Agent buttons

items.title: Plan button on features uses feature's own status, ignoring derivedStatus

narrative.Action: In FeatureCard's action bar and InspectorPanel's feature actions, the Plan button visibility must use the feature's own tracker status (issue.status), not derivedStatus. A feature in 'Todo' state in Rally should show Plan even if derivedStatus is 'in_progress' because children are active. This aligns with 'plan at Feature, execute at Story' — feature planning state is independent of child progress.

subItems.title: Feature with status='todo' and derivedStatus='in_progress' shows Plan button in FeatureCard action bar
Feature with status='todo' and derivedStatus='in_progress' shows Plan button in InspectorPanel
Feature with status='closed' does NOT show Plan button regardless of derivedStatus

items.title: Add getChildIssues() method to IssueTracker interface

narrative.Action: Add getChildIssues(parentId: string): Promise<Issue[]> to the IssueTracker interface in src/lib/tracker/interface.ts. Add a default no-op implementation that returns empty array for trackers that don't support hierarchy (GitHub, Linear). Only Rally will have a real implementation.

subItems.title: IssueTracker interface has getChildIssues(parentId: string): Promise<Issue[]> method
GitHub and Linear tracker implementations return empty array from getChildIssues()
TypeScript compiles cleanly — all IssueTracker implementations satisfy the new method

narrative.Action: Implement getChildIssues() in src/lib/tracker/rally.ts. Given a feature ID (e.g., 'F1234'), query Rally API for HierarchicalRequirement and Defect artifacts whose PortfolioItem field matches the feature. Return normalized Issue[] objects. Use existing Rally query patterns from listIssues().

subItems.title: RallyTracker.getChildIssues('F1234') returns all HierarchicalRequirement/Defect children of feature F1234
Returned issues are normalized to Issue[] format with parentRef set to the feature ID
Returns empty array when feature has no children or feature ID is invalid

items.title: Planning prompt detects Rally Feature and includes child story context

narrative.Action: Enhance spawn-planning-session.ts to detect when the issue is a Rally Feature (artifactType includes 'PortfolioItem'). When detected, call getChildIssues() to fetch child stories and include them in the planning prompt as context. The planning agent sees: feature description + list of child stories (ID, title, status, description). This uses the existing planning flow — no new planning mode.

subItems.title: spawn-planning-session detects Rally Feature via artifactType and fetches child stories via getChildIssues()
Planning prompt includes a 'Child Stories' section listing each child's ID, title, status, and description
Non-feature issues (stories, defects) skip the child story fetch — no behavior change for existing flow

items.title: Write FEATURE-CONTEXT.md to .planning/ for story workspaces

narrative.Action: When spawning a story workspace that belongs to a Rally Feature, inject .planning/FEATURE-CONTEXT.md containing: the parent feature's title, architectural decisions from the feature's vBRIEF narratives, and cross-story dependency notes. This gives story work agents awareness of the broader feature plan. Read from the feature's workspace at workspaces/feature-<featureId>/.planning/plan.vbrief.json. Also add FEATURE_CONTEXT injection to work-agent-prompt.ts readPlanningContext().

subItems.title: .planning/FEATURE-CONTEXT.md is written to story workspace containing parent feature title and vBRIEF narratives
work-agent-prompt.ts readPlanningContext() reads FEATURE-CONTEXT.md and injects it into the work agent prompt
Story workspaces without a parent feature skip FEATURE-CONTEXT.md — no error, no empty file

items.title: Feature-level vBRIEF supports cross-story dependency edges

narrative.Action: Document and validate that feature-level vBRIEF plans can include edges between story items using blocks/informs/invalidates/suggests types. The vBRIEF types already support edges (VBriefEdge in types.ts). Ensure the planning prompt for features instructs the planning agent to include cross-story dependency edges when relevant. These edges are written to the vBRIEF but NOT enforced by Cloister — that is deferred to a future issue.

subItems.title: Feature planning prompt instructs agent to include cross-story dependency edges in the vBRIEF
VBriefViewer displays edges from feature-level plans without errors
Edges are NOT enforced by Cloister — no changes to cloister/service.ts ordering logic

items.title: Tests for FeatureCard action bar rendering and chip interactions

narrative.Action: Write component tests for FeatureCard action bar: Plan/See Plan chip renders correctly based on hasPlan state, vBRIEF chip opens VBriefDialog, Tasks chip opens BeadsTasksPanel, no Start Agent button present. Test derivedStatus gate: feature with derivedStatus='in_progress' and status='todo' still shows Plan.

subItems.title: Test: FeatureCard renders Plan chip when feature status is todo and hasPlan is false
Test: FeatureCard renders See Plan chip when hasPlan is true
Test: FeatureCard does NOT render Start Agent button
Test: Feature with derivedStatus='in_progress' and status='todo' shows Plan button

items.title: Tests for FeatureCard and CompactChildCard click-to-select behavior

narrative.Action: Write component tests: FeatureCard title click sets selectedIssue, chevron click toggles expand without changing selection. CompactChildCard row click sets selectedIssue, identifier link click doesn't propagate to row selection. Both show selected visual state.

subItems.title: Test: FeatureCard title click fires onSelect, chevron click fires onToggle
Test: CompactChildCard row click fires onSelect, identifier link click does not
Test: Both components show selected visual state when isSelected prop is true

narrative.Action: Write component tests: InspectorPanel with a feature issue shows Plan/vBRIEF/Tasks but not Start Agent. InspectorPanel with a story issue still shows Start Agent (regression check).

subItems.title: Test: InspectorPanel with PortfolioItem issue shows Plan, vBRIEF, Tasks buttons
Test: InspectorPanel with PortfolioItem issue does NOT show Start Agent button
Test: InspectorPanel with story issue still shows Start Agent button (regression)

items.title: Tests for getChildIssues() interface and Rally implementation

narrative.Action: Write unit tests for getChildIssues(): Rally implementation returns normalized children for a feature, returns empty for non-feature. GitHub/Linear implementations return empty array. Test that returned issues have parentRef set correctly.

subItems.title: Test: RallyTracker.getChildIssues() returns children with correct parentRef for a known feature
Test: RallyTracker.getChildIssues() returns empty array for non-existent feature
Test: GitHubTracker.getChildIssues() returns empty array

items.title: Tests for feature-aware planning prompt and context injection

narrative.Action: Write integration tests: planning prompt for a Rally Feature includes child story context. FEATURE-CONTEXT.md is written correctly for story workspaces with a parent feature. Work agent prompt includes FEATURE_CONTEXT section. Non-feature planning and non-child stories are unaffected.

subItems.title: Test: Planning prompt for Rally Feature includes 'Child Stories' section with child issue details
Test: FEATURE-CONTEXT.md is written with parent feature title and vBRIEF narratives
Test: Non-feature issue planning prompt does NOT include child story context (regression)
